figures.caption: Figure 1: Real-world deployment of the proposed LLMguided deterministic safety agent on a redundant dualRK3588 SoC platform.

Figure 2: The hierarchical framework of the safety agent. Step 1: LLM-based safety formalization for constraint extraction (𝐶𝐴,𝐶𝐵). Step 2: Multidimensional perception alignment including spatial mapping (𝑆𝑠𝑎𝑓 𝑒 ) and temporal WCET analysis (𝑇𝑠𝑡𝑜𝑝 ). Step 3: The Perception-Compute-Control architecture.

Figure 4: Visual representation of the three experimental scenarios utilized in the performance evaluation, with corresponding data metrics detailed in Table 1. Red floor regions denote pre-defined danger zones (𝑅𝑅𝑂𝐼 ), and green bounding boxes represent detected human subjects. (Left) Scenario 1: A standard industrial workspace with a single worker under clear overhead visibility. (Middle) Scenario 2: A challenging environment where the worker is partially obscured. (Right) Scenario 3: A complex spatial layout featuring two or more workers in a multi-target configuration.
Table 1: Latency Breakdown and WCET Analysis across Multiple Scenarios (ms)

Figure 3: Hardware architecture of the integrated PPC system. The design features a symmetric dual-node redundancy centered on the Rockchip RK3588 SoC, facilitating an end-to-end pipeline from raw sensor acquisition to deterministic hardware execution via Ext. IO.
